description: This figure illustrates the microstructure of as-hot-rolled Fe–10Mn–10Al–0.7C low-density steel. Subfigure (a) is an optical microscopy (OM) micrograph captured on the RD-ND (rolling direction-normal direction) plane, with a 50 μm scale bar, exhibiting a typical banded structure composed of alternating α-ferrite and γ-austenite strips formed during hot rolling. Subfigure (b) is an OM micrograph of the RD-TD (rolling direction-transverse direction) plane with a 50 μm scale bar, showing an inhomogeneous microstructure consisting of large continuous α-phase dominated regions and γ-phase dominated regions. Subfigure (c) is a scanning electron microscopy (SEM) micrograph of the RD-TD plane with a 2 μm scale bar, clearly identifying nanoscale κ-carbides precipitating along α/γ interphase boundaries and α/α grain boundaries. EDS characterization confirms α phase is Al-rich, γ phase is Mn- and C-rich, and κ-carbide is rich in Mn and Al. This figure provides the baseline initial microstructure of the hot-rolled specimen, supporting subsequent analysis of microstructure evolution and property variation during post annealing treatments.
